title: calendula suffruticosa subsp. maderensis
intro: Calendula suffruticosa subsp. maderensis, also known as the Madeiran marigold, is a subspecies of the calendula suffruticosa, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. This plant is known for its bright yellow and orange flowers, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal and therapeutic properties. The medicinal properties of Calendula suffruticosa subsp. maderensis include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce inflammation, and fight off infections. The health benefits of this plant include promoting wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system, and it can be prepared into various herbal preparations such as infusions, salves, and ointments.
